ffmpeg.program_description: ffmpeg - multimedia framework for video/audio conversion and processing

convert.description: Converts media from one format to another
commands: ffmpeg -i input.mov -c:v libx264 -preset medium -crf 23 -c:a aac -b:a 192k output.mp4  # standard MP4 conversion [citation:1]
ffmpeg -i input.mov -c:v libx264 -crf 18 -preset slow -c:a copy output.mp4  # high quality [citation:2]
ffmpeg -i input.avi -c:v libx264 -crf 23 -c:a aac -b:a 128k output.mp4  # AVI to MP4 [citation:2]
ffmpeg -i input.mkv -c copy output.mp4  # remux without re-encoding [citation:2]
ffmpeg -i input.mov -c:v prores_ks -profile:v 3 -pix_fmt yuv422p10 -c:a pcm_s24le output.mov  # ProRes HQ for editing [citation:1]
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -c:v libx265 -crf 28 -preset medium -c:a libopus output.mkv  # H.265/HEVC [citation:4]
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -c:v libvpx-vp9 -crf 30 -b:v 0 -c:a libopus output.webm  # VP9 for web [citation:6]

convert-to-mp3.description: Extracts audio from video and saves as MP3
commands: ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -vn -acodec libmp3lame -ab 192k audio.mp3  # high quality MP3 [citation:6]
ffmpeg -i input.flv -vn -ab 320k output.mp3  # highest quality [citation:7]
ffmpeg -i input.m4a -acodec libmp3lame -vn -b:a 320k output.mp3  # convert M4A to MP3 [citation:6]
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -vn -codec:a libmp3lame -q:a 2 audio.mp3  # variable bitrate (0-9, lower is better) [citation:2]

extract-audio.description: Extracts audio from video without conversion
commands: ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -vn -acodec copy audio.aac  # extract original audio [citation:7]
ffmpeg -i input.mkv -vn -c:a copy audio.mka  # extract from MKV
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -vn -c:a pcm_s16le audio.wav  # extract as WAV
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -vn -acodec aac -ab 128k audio.m4a  # extract as AAC

trim.description: Trims video to a specific time range
commands: ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -ss 00:01:30 -to 00:02:30 -c copy output.mp4  # fast trim (keyframe accurate) [citation:2]
ffmpeg -ss 00:00:10 -i input.mp4 -t 15 -c copy output.mp4  # 15 seconds from 10 sec mark [citation:4]
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -ss 00:00:05 -to 00:00:12 -c:v libx264 -crf 20 -c:a aac output.mp4  # frame-accurate (slower) [citation:2]
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -ss 30 -c copy output.mp4  # remove first 30 seconds [citation:7]
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -to 100 -c copy output.mp4  # keep first 100 seconds [citation:7]
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -vf scale=1280:720 output.mp4  # specific dimensions [citation:2]
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -vf "scale=1280:-2" output.mp4  # preserve aspect ratio [citation:2]
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -vf "scale=1080:-1,pad=1080:1080:(ow-iw)/2:(oh-ih)/2" output.mp4  # square for Instagram [citation:1]
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -vf "scale=w=1080:h=1920:force_original_aspect_ratio=decrease,pad=1080:1920:(ow-iw)/2:(oh-ih)/2" output.mp4  # vertical with padding [citation:9]
ffmpeg -i input.avi -vf scale=320:240 output.avi  # simple resize [citation:7]
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -filter:v "setpts=0.5*PTS" -filter:a "atempo=2" output.mp4  # 2x speed [citation:1]
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -filter:a "atempo=0.5" -vn output.aac  # half speed audio [citation:7]
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -filter:v "setpts=2*PTS" -filter:a "atempo=0.5" output.mp4  # half speed
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -filter:a "atempo=1.5" -vn output.mp3  # 1.5x speed audio

merge.description: Merges multiple video files into one
commands: printf "file '1.mp4'\nfile '2.mp4'\n" > list.txt && ffmpeg -f concat -safe 0 -i list.txt -c copy output.mp4  # lossless merge [citation:1]
ffmpeg -i "concat:file1.mp3|file2.mp3" -acodec copy output.mp3  # merge MP3s [citation:7]
ffmpeg -i opening.mp4 -i content.mp4 -i ending.mp4 -filter_complex "[0:v][0:a][1:v][1:a][2:v][2:a]concat=n=3:v=1:a=1" output.mp4  # complex merge [citation:7]
ffmpeg -f concat -i mylist.txt -c copy output.mp4  # with file list [citation:6]

add-audio.description: Adds or replaces audio track in video
commands: ffmpeg -i video.mp4 -i audio.mp3 -map 0:v -map 1:a -c:v copy -c:a aac -shortest output.mp4  # replace audio [citation:9]
ffmpeg -i video.mp4 -i audio.wav -map 0:v -map 1:a -c:v copy -c:a aac output.mp4  # add audio
ffmpeg -i draft.mp4 -i studio_mix.wav -map 0:v -map 1:a -c:v copy -c:a aac final.mp4  # swap with studio audio [citation:1]
ffmpeg -i video.avi -i audio.mp3 -map 0.0:1 -map 1:0 -vcodec copy -acodec copy output.avi  # mux with mapping [citation:7]
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -vf fps=1 frame_%04d.png  # 1 frame per second [citation:2]
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -ss 00:00:05 -vframes 1 -s 128x128 image.png  # single frame at timestamp [citation:6]
ffmpeg -i input.mpg image%d.jpg  # all frames [citation:7]
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -r 1 image%d.jpg  # 1 image per second [citation:7]
ffmpeg -i video.mp4 -vf "select='eq(pict_type,I)'" -fps_mode vfr -frame_pts 1 keyframe_%06d.jpg  # extract keyframes [citation:11]
ffmpeg -i input.mov -vframes 1 -s 320x240 -ss 10 thumb.jpg  # thumbnail at 10 seconds [citation:7]
ffmpeg -i scene.mp4 -ss 00:00:02 -t 3 -vf "fps=12,scale=480:-1:flags=lanczos" -c:v gif -loop 0 output.gif  # optimized GIF [citation:1]
ffmpeg -i video.mp4 -vf 'scale=-1:1000' -r 15 output.gif  # scale and framerate [citation:6]
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -vf "fps=10,scale=320:-1:flags=lanczos" -c:v gif -loop 0 output.gif  # small GIF

prores.description: Converts to ProRes for professional editing
commands: ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -c:v prores_ks -profile:v 3 -pix_fmt yuv422p10 -c:a pcm_s24le output.mov  # ProRes HQ [citation:1]
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -c:v prores_ks -profile:v 0 -c:a copy output.mov  # Proxy (low quality)
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -c:v prores_ks -profile:v 1 -c:a copy output.mov  # LT (medium)
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -c:v prores_ks -profile:v 2 -c:a copy output.mov  # Standard
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -c:v prores_ks -profile:v 4 -c:a pcm_s24le output.mov  # 4444 (VFX/compositing) [citation:10]

normalize-audio.description: Normalizes audio loudness to streaming standards
commands: ffmpeg -i input.wav -af loudnorm=I=-14:TP=-2:LRA=11 output.wav  # standard loudness [citation:1]
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -af loudnorm=linear=true:measured_i=-16.92:measured_tp=0.55:measured_lra=24.20 -ar 48000 -c:a libopus -ac 2 output.mka  # two-pass loudnorm [citation:4]
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -af "volume=1.16dB" output.mp4  # simple volume boost [citation:4]

subtitles.description: Burns subtitles into video or extracts them
commands: ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -vf "subtitles=subs.srt,format=yuv420p" -c:v libx264 -crf 20 -c:a copy output.mp4  # burn SRT [citation:2]
ffmpeg -i input_video.mp4 -vf ass=sub.ass output.mp4  # burn ASS subtitles [citation:6]
ffmpeg -i foo.mkv -c:s srt foo.srt  # extract subtitles as SRT [citation:8]
ffmpeg -i foo.mkv foo.ssa  # extract subtitles as SSA [citation:8]
ffmpeg -i video.mkv -map 0:s -c:s copy subs.mkv  # extract all subtitle tracks [citation:4]
ffmpeg -i input.mp4  # basic info [citation:4]
ffprobe -v quiet -print_format json -show_format -show_streams input.mp4  # JSON format [citation:7]
ffprobe -v error -show_entries format=duration -of default=noprint_wrappers=1:nokey=1 input.mp4  # get duration [citation:11]
ffprobe -v error -select_streams v:0 -show_entries stream=width,height -of csv=s=x:p=0 input.mp4  # get dimensions [citation:11]
ffprobe -v error -select_streams v:0 -show_entries stream=codec_name -of default=nokey=1:noprint_wrappers=1 input.mp4  # get codec [citation:7]
ffmpeg -i input.mkv 2>&1 | grep "Stream #0"  # show only stream info [citation:4]

slideshow.description: Creates video from image sequence or slideshow
commands: ffmpeg -framerate 24 -i img%03d.png output.mp4  # image sequence [citation:5]
ffmpeg -loop 1 -i img.jpg -t 30 -c:v libx264 -pix_fmt yuv420p output.mp4  # static image with duration [citation:5]
ffmpeg -framerate 1/5 -i img%03d.png -c:v libx264 -vf fps=25 -pix_fmt yuv420p output.mp4  # 5 sec per image [citation:5]
ffmpeg -framerate 10 -pattern_type glob -i '*.jpg' -c:v libx264 -pix_fmt yuv420p output.mp4  # use glob pattern [citation:5]
ffmpeg -loop 1 -i img.jpg -i audio.wav -c:v libx264 -c:a aac -shortest output.mp4  # image with audio [citation:5]
ffmpeg -f x11grab -video_size 1920x1080 -i :0.0 -vframes 1 screen.png  # screenshot [citation:8]
ffmpeg -f x11grab -video_size 1920x1080 -framerate 25 -i :0.0 -c:v ffvhuff screen.mkv  # lossless screencast [citation:8]
ffmpeg -f x11grab -video_size 1920x1080 -framerate 25 -i :0.0 -f alsa -i default -c:v libx264 -preset ultrafast -c:a aac screen.mp4  # with audio [citation:8]
ffmpeg -f v4l2 -video_size 640x480 -i /dev/video0 -c:v libx264 -preset ultrafast webcam.mp4  # webcam capture [citation:8]

webm.description: Converts video to WebM VP9 format
commands: ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -c:v libvpx-vp9 -crf 30 -b:v 0 -c:a libopus -vbr on output.webm  # VP9 conversion [citation:6]
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -c:v libvpx-vp9 -crf 28 -b:v 0 -c:a libopus output.webm  # high quality VP9
ffmpeg -i input.webm output.mp4  # WebM to MP4 [citation:6]

printf "file '1.mp4'
file '2.mp4'
file '3.mp4'
" > list.txt && ffmpeg -f concat -safe 0 -i list.txt -c copy output.mp4  # lossless concat [citation:6]
ffmpeg -f concat -i mylist.txt -c copy output.mp4  # with text file [citation:2]

filter-complex.description: Uses complex filter for advanced operations
commands: ffmpeg -i screen.mp4 -i facecam.mp4 -filter_complex "[0:v][1:v]overlay=W-w-20:H-h-20" -c:v libx264 output.mp4  # picture-in-picture [citation:1]
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -filter_complex "[0:v]split=2[s0][s1];[s0]scale=1920:1080[out1];[s1]scale=720:1280[out2]" -map [out1] -map [out2] output.mp4  # split and scale [citation:9]
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -filter_complex "[0:v]crop=w:h:x:y" output.mp4  # crop with complex filter

image2video.description: Creates video from a single image or image sequence
commands: ffmpeg -loop 1 -i img.jpg -c:v libx264 -t 30 -pix_fmt yuv420p out.mp4  # single image with duration [citation:5]
ffmpeg -framerate 24 -i img%03d.png -pix_fmt yuv420p output.mp4  # sequential images
ffmpeg -start_number 126 -i img%03d.png -pix_fmt yuv420p out.mp4  # start at specific image [citation:5]
